one_line_takeaway: A telecom-focused survey of LLM hallucination causes and mitigations, plus a case study showing DPO fine-tuning (17.4%->21.0% correct rate) and a diffusion-DRL-gated mobile-edge MoE lift 'hallucination-aware QoE'; a reliability paper, not a security one.
key_finding: DPO fine-tuning raised the Falcon-7b-Instruct correct rate from 17.4% to 21.0% (reported as a 20.6% relative improvement); the larger Falcon-11b reached 48.7% correct rate. The diffusion-DRL MoE gating network achieved higher average hallucination-aware QoE reward (~400) than PPO (~380) and clearly beat mobile-only, edge-only, random, and round-robin routing, which suffer congestion or lack dynamic, request-conditioned expert selection.
core_contribution: A survey-plus-case-study (IEEE-magazine style) on hallucination in LLM-empowered communications/telecom. The paper's OWN contributions are three: (1) a systematic mapping of hallucination causes onto the LLM lifecycle (dataset collection, pre-training, SFT, preference alignment, inference) and a taxonomy of mitigation strategies split into model-based (hallucination-detection datasets, RAG, prompt engineering / CoT, dataset dedup, factuality-enhanced decoding) and system-based (federated learning, mixture-of-experts, secure multi-party computation); (2) a comparative review of existing LLM-for-communications schemes (TelecomGPT, WirelessLLM, LLM-SemCom, optimization-formulation LLMs, etc.) summarized in a table of which mitigation each adopts; (3) a concrete case study: a Telecom-oriented LLM that publishes a new Telecom hallucination dataset (TeleQnA-Hallucination) and combines DPO fine-tuning (model side) with a mobile-edge MoE architecture routed by a diffusion-model DRL gating network (system side) to maximize a 'hallucination-aware QoE'.
threat_model: No genuine security threat model is proposed. This is a reliability/trustworthiness paper: 'hallucination' means fact-conflicting, input-conflicting, or context-conflicting generation, treated as a quality/QoE problem, not an adversary-driven one. Security terms do appear but are peripheral and mostly in cited-work or future-work framing: adversarial/backdoor/data-poisoning and 'hallucination attacks' (Yao et al.) are listed as one of several hallucination causes; SMPC is mentioned as a mitigation that 'indirectly' reduces poisoning risk; a Future-Directions subsection lists physical/network/application-layer attack surfaces but proposes no defense. The feed abstract snippet ('SMPC prevents any single entity... minimizing the risk of data poisoning attacks') is one sentence from the mitigation-survey section and is NOT the paper's contribution. Forcing a security frame here would misrepresent the work.
limitations: Magazine-format survey/case-study, not a rigorous empirical study: no ablations isolating DPO vs LoRA vs MoE contributions, and absolute correct rates are low (17-49%), so '20.6% improvement' is a modest ~3.6-percentage-point gain on a weak base. The 'novel hybrid' is an integration of off-the-shelf components (LoRA, DPO, MoE, diffusion-DRL gating from the authors' prior work) rather than a new algorithm. Only Falcon-series models and the single TeleQnA-derived dataset are evaluated; the rejected/chosen labels are derived mechanically from multiple-choice options. QoE reward weighting is not fully specified, and MoE results are shown only as reward curves without a per-component breakdown. Security topics are named but neither modeled nor defended.

datasets_used: TeleQnA (10000 Q&A items, base dataset cited as ref [11])
TeleQnA-Hallucination -- the authors' own DPO-reformatted dataset (8000 training / 2000 test, chosen vs rejected answers), published on GitHub
quantitative_results: Falcon-7b-Instruct without DPO: 17.4% correct rate, 17.5 generation quality, ~86 tokens/sec, ~1743 J per 1000 tokens
Falcon-7b-Instruct with DPO: 21.0% correct rate, 24.8 generation quality (reported 20.6% relative improvement over the no-DPO baseline)
Falcon-11b (~11B params, edge expert): 48.7% correct rate, 30.5 generation quality, ~72 tokens/sec, ~$0.5 per hour
MoE diffusion-DRL gating average reward ~400 vs PPO ~380 (Fig. 5b)
Cited (not the authors' own): LLM-SemCom [3] reduces hallucination rate by up to 30%; RAG-based optimization formulation [6] gives correct modeling in >80% of cases
baselines_compared: Model-side ablation: Falcon-7b with vs without DPO; Falcon-11b as larger-model reference
